daily_brief: Day 36 of the US-Israeli war on Iran saw significant escalation as Tehran claimed to have downed two American warplanes—an F-15E Strike Eagle over southwestern Iran and an A-10 Warthog over the Gulf—with one crew member still missing and being hunted by Iranian forces [BBC, France 24, Globe and Mail]. The incidents challenge US claims of total air superiority, while Tehran rejected a 48-hour ceasefire proposal conveyed through intermediaries [The Hindu]. The Strait of Hormuz saw its first Western commercial crossings since the conflict began, with French and Japanese vessels navigating Iranian-approved routes [RFI, BBC]. In Europe, Hungary's upcoming April 12 election could unseat Viktor Orbán after 16 years, with opposition leader Peter Magyar polling at 58% versus Orbán's 35% [BBC]. Meanwhile, the Artemis II crew passed the halfway point to the Moon, releasing striking images of Earth [BBC, Al Jazeera]. Burkina Faso's military ruler Ibrahim Traoré declared democracy 'not for us,' following a Human Rights Watch report documenting over 1,800 civilian deaths since 2023 [BBC, The Guardian, RFI].

top_stories.headline: Iran Downs Two US Warplanes; Search Underway for Missing Crew Member
bullets: Iran claimed it shot down a US F-15E Strike Eagle over Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad province and an A-10 Warthog over the Gulf, marking the first US aircraft losses since the war began five weeks ago
US special forces rescued two crew members; one F-15E crew member remains missing with both Iranian forces and US search teams racing to locate them
Two US Black Hawk helicopters involved in rescue operations were hit by Iranian fire but remained airborne and exited Iranian airspace
Tehran rejected a US 48-hour ceasefire proposal conveyed through an unnamed intermediary on April 1

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Focused on complexity of combat search-and-rescue operations and historical context

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized this as major escalation following Trump's claims of having 'decimated' Iran

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Highlighted Iranian celebration and framed as contradiction of US air superiority claims

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Noted incidents challenge assertions from Trump and Hegseth about total control of skies

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Led with ceasefire rejection and included criticism from international law experts

top_stories.headline: First Western Commercial Ships Cross Strait of Hormuz Since War Began
bullets: A French-owned CMA CGM container ship (Malta-flagged Kribi) and a Japanese MOL vessel crossed the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, the first Western European commercial crossings since the conflict started
Ships navigated via an Iranian-approved route through its waters, dubbed the 'Tehran Toll Booth,' or hugged unusually close to Oman's coast
Before the war, approximately 20% of global oil and liquefied natural gas passed through the Strait
Iran has stated 'non-hostile vessels' may use the waterway, though several ships have been attacked during the conflict

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Straightforward reporting on the crossing with context on ongoing conflict risks

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Detailed the specific routes taken and ship tracking data

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Brief mention within broader conflict coverage

top_stories.headline: Hungary's Orbán Faces Potential Defeat as Opposition Leads by 23 Points Before April 12 Election
bullets: Opposition Tisza party leader Peter Magyar leads Viktor Orbán's Fidesz 58% to 35% in latest polling ahead of the April 12 parliamentary election
Orbán, Europe's longest-serving leader, has been forced to campaign extensively after giving few rallies in past elections
A defeat would impact the broader European populist movement, as Orbán has been a model for nationalist parties across the continent
Orbán has maintained relationships with both Trump and Putin while opposing EU support for Ukraine

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Provided extensive context on Orbán's regional and international significance

top_stories.headline: Artemis II Crew Passes Halfway Point to Moon, Releases First High-Resolution Earth Images
bullets: NASA's Artemis II spacecraft reached 142,000 miles from Earth and 132,000 miles from the Moon approximately two days and five hours after launch
Commander Reid Wiseman captured images showing Earth with the Atlantic Ocean, aurora at both poles, and Venus visible in frame
The crew successfully completed a trans-lunar injection burn that set them on trajectory toward the Moon
The mission aims to take humans farther from Earth than ever before as part of NASA's return to lunar exploration

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Detailed technical coverage including specific distances and crew reactions

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Brief video coverage emphasizing historic nature of mission

top_stories.headline: Burkina Faso's Military Ruler Declares Democracy 'Not For Us' Amid Reports of 1,800+ Civilian Deaths
bullets: Captain Ibrahim Traoré told state television that Burkinabè people should 'forget about democracy,' citing Libya's post-intervention instability as justification
The junta banned all political parties in January 2026 and extended Traoré's rule until 2029 after initially promising elections by July 2024
Human Rights Watch reported that the army, civilian auxiliaries (VDP), and jihadist groups have killed at least 1,837 civilians since January 2023
HRW called for the International Criminal Court to open a preliminary examination into war crimes and crimes against humanity

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Led with Traoré's anti-democracy statements and Libya comparison

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Included context on Traoré's anti-Western rhetoric and Thomas Sankara comparisons

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Focused on HRW report findings and called for ICC investigation

additional_stories.headline: Israel launches new strikes on Beirut and southern Lebanese city of Tyre, injuring several
French court overturns police ban on major Muslim gathering near Paris, event proceeds
Russia launches large-scale drone and missile attack on Ukraine during Orthodox Easter period, killing six
Venezuela and US normalize relations three months after Maduro's removal
Uganda receives first US deportation flight under third-country agreement
WHO warns of attacks on Iranian health facilities including Pasteur Institute, launches regional aid appeal
Iran executes two more individuals linked to opposition group PMOI amid ongoing conflict
US company secures major copper and cobalt mining deal in Democratic Republic of Congo
Canada and China sign agreement to deepen financial-sector ties during ministerial visit to Beijing
Italy's Uffizi Galleries confirms cyber-attack but denies security systems compromised
